Matt Hunzinger

My Development Setup For Linux

I’m sure you’ve seen lists like these a thousand times before but hear me out, you may find this list useful. After finally ditching Windows in favor of Arch Linux, here are some of the tools I’ve found:

Atom, in my opinion, is the best text editor because of the extensions, themes, and the fact that it’s open source. My favorite extensions are Minimap and Snippets.

Watch HTTP Server for live reloading while editing.

Jekyll for locally testing before pushing to Github pages.

Yaourt for downloading packages from the AUR.

BSPWM is a great window manager and really improves productivity with shortcuts. However, I wouldn’t recommend this if you’re just getting started with Linux.

GNOME is a great desktop environment that’s very minimalistic and allows for a variety of themes and extensions. The Arc theme is by far my favorite.

And of course telnet towel.blinkenlights.nl so I can watch Star Wars in my terminal.
